title: aspidium subtriphyllum
intro: Aspidium subtriphyllum, also known as the Deer Tongue Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Aspleniaceae. It is primarily found in tropical regions of Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Aspidium subtriphyllum include reducing fever, alleviating headaches, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Aspidium subtriphyllum include infusions and decoctions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water to create a medicinal tea.
